title: Fish-tail and tear-out edge study
instructions: Mark the visual evidence, not the desired saw. Use usable_edge for the last sound material, defect_onset for fish-tail, wane, or tear-out, and lighting_limit when the image cannot support a reliable call.

boards.focus: Best documented example: fish tails on both sides; 4.2 boundary case.
Close fish tail; retain at least 200 mm for downstream optimisation.
Fish tail selected for trimming while retaining 200 mm.
Tear-out near trim side; compare acceptable wane against a true defect.
Fish tail missed by the operator/PLC decision.
Large fish tail; 200 mm downstream allowance is important.
Large remaining fish tail; test whether its onset is visually detectable.
Origin-side fish tail and reversed-feed example.
Severe tear-out just before the 5.4 saw position.
Fish-tail allowance example with the 200 mm margin.
